00023  * OR SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION)
00024  * HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, STRICT

00026  * OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF

00029 
00030 
00031 /*
00032  * Code to load an ELF-format executable into the current address space.
00033  *
00034  * It makes the following address space calls:
00035  *    - first, as_define_region once for each segment of the program;
00036  *    - then, as_prepare_load;
00037  *    - then it loads each chunk of the program;
00038  *    - finally, as_complete_load.
00039  *
00040  * This gives the VM code enough flexibility to deal with even grossly
00041  * mis-linked executables if that proves desirable. Under normal
00042  * circumstances, as_prepare_load and as_complete_load probably don't
00043  * need to do anything.
00044  *
00045  * If you wanted to support memory-mapped executables you would need
00046  * to rearrange this to map each segment.
00047  *
00048  * To support dynamically linked executables with shared libraries
00049  * you'd need to change this to load the "ELF interpreter" (dynamic
00050  * linker). And you'd have to write a dynamic linker...
00051  */
00052 
00053 #include <types.h>
00054 #include <kern/errno.h>
00055 #include <lib.h>
00056 #include <uio.h>
00057 #include <thread.h>
00058 #include <current.h>
00059 #include <addrspace.h>
00060 #include <vnode.h>
00061 #include <elf.h>
00062 
00063 /*
00064  * Load a segment at virtual address VADDR. The segment in memory
00065  * extends from VADDR up to (but not including) VADDR+MEMSIZE. The
00066  * segment on disk is located at file offset OFFSET and has length
00067  * FILESIZE.
00068  *
00069  * FILESIZE may be less than MEMSIZE; if so the remaining portion of
00070  * the in-memory segment should be zero-filled.
00071  *
00072  * Note that uiomove will catch it if someone tries to load an
00073  * executable whose load address is in kernel space. If you should
00074  * change this code to not use uiomove, be sure to check for this case
00075  * explicitly.
00076  */
00077 static
00078 int
00079 load_segment(struct vnode *v, off_t offset, vaddr_t vaddr, 
00080              size_t memsize, size_t filesize,
00081              int is_executable)
00082 {
00083         struct iovec iov;
00084         struct uio u;
00085         int result;
00086 
00087         if (filesize > memsize) {
00088                 kprintf("ELF: warning: segment filesize > segment memsize\n");
00089                 filesize = memsize;
00090         }
00091 
00092         DEBUG(DB_EXEC, "ELF: Loading %lu bytes to 0x%lx\n", 
00093               (unsigned long) filesize, (unsigned long) vaddr);
00094 
00095         iov.iov_ubase = (userptr_t)vaddr;
00096         iov.iov_len = memsize;           // length of the memory space
00097         u.uio_iov = &iov;
00098         u.uio_iovcnt = 1;
00099         u.uio_resid = filesize;          // amount to read from the file
00100         u.uio_offset = offset;
00101         u.uio_segflg = is_executable ? UIO_USERISPACE : UIO_USERSPACE;
00102         u.uio_rw = UIO_READ;
00103         u.uio_space = curthread->t_addrspace;
00104 
00105         result = VOP_READ(v, &u);
00106         if (result) {
00107                 return result;
00108         }
00109 
00110         if (u.uio_resid != 0) {
00111                 /* short read; problem with executable? */
00112                 kprintf("ELF: short read on segment - file truncated?\n");
00113                 return ENOEXEC;
00114         }
00115 
00116         /*
00117          * If memsize > filesize, the remaining space should be
00118          * zero-filled. There is no need to do this explicitly,
00119          * because the VM system should provide pages that do not
00120          * contain other processes' data, i.e., are already zeroed.
00121          *
00122          * During development of your VM system, it may have bugs that
00123          * cause it to (maybe only sometimes) not provide zero-filled
00124          * pages, which can cause user programs to fail in strange
00125          * ways. Explicitly zeroing program BSS may help identify such
00126          * bugs, so the following disabled code is provided as a
00127          * diagnostic tool. Note that it must be disabled again before
00128          * you submit your code for grading.
00129          */
00130 #if 0
00131         {
00132                 size_t fillamt;
00133 
00134                 fillamt = memsize - filesize;
00135                 if (fillamt > 0) {
00136                         DEBUG(DB_EXEC, "ELF: Zero-filling %lu more bytes\n", 
00137                               (unsigned long) fillamt);
00138                         u.uio_resid += fillamt;
00139                         result = uiomovezeros(fillamt, &u);
00140                 }
00141         }
00142 #endif
00143         
00144         return result;
00145 }
00146 
00147 /*
00148  * Load an ELF executable user program into the current address space.
00149  *
00150  * Returns the entry point (initial PC) for the program in ENTRYPOINT.
00151  */
00152 int
00153 load_elf(struct vnode *v, vaddr_t *entrypoint)
00154 {
00155         Elf_Ehdr eh;   /* Executable header */
00156         Elf_Phdr ph;   /* "Program header" = segment header */
00157         int result, i;
00158         struct iovec iov;
00159         struct uio ku;
00160 
00161         /*
00162          * Read the executable header from offset 0 in the file.
00163          */
00164 
00165         uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, &eh, sizeof(eh), 0, UIO_READ);
00166         result = VOP_READ(v, &ku);
00167         if (result) {
00168                 return result;
00169         }
00170 
00171         if (ku.uio_resid != 0) {
00172                 /* short read; problem with executable? */
00173                 kprintf("ELF: short read on header - file truncated?\n");
00174                 return ENOEXEC;
00175         }
00176 
00177         /*
00178          * Check to make sure it's a 32-bit ELF-version-1 executable
00179          * for our processor type. If it's not, we can't run it.
00180          *
00181          * Ignore EI_OSABI and EI_ABIVERSION - properly, we should
00182          * define our own, but that would require tinkering with the
00183          * linker to have it emit our magic numbers instead of the
00184          * default ones. (If the linker even supports these fields,
00185          * which were not in the original elf spec.)
00186          */
00187 
00188         if (eh.e_ident[EI_MAG0] != ELFMAG0 ||
00189             eh.e_ident[EI_MAG1] != ELFMAG1 ||
00190             eh.e_ident[EI_MAG2] != ELFMAG2 ||
00191             eh.e_ident[EI_MAG3] != ELFMAG3 ||
00192             eh.e_ident[EI_CLASS] != ELFCLASS32 ||
00193             eh.e_ident[EI_DATA] != ELFDATA2MSB ||
00194             eh.e_ident[EI_VERSION] != EV_CURRENT ||
00195             eh.e_version != EV_CURRENT ||
00196             eh.e_type!=ET_EXEC ||
00197             eh.e_machine!=EM_MACHINE) {
00198                 return ENOEXEC;
00199         }
00200 
00201         /*
00202          * Go through the list of segments and set up the address space.
00203          *
00204          * Ordinarily there will be one code segment, one read-only
00205          * data segment, and one data/bss segment, but there might
00206          * conceivably be more. You don't need to support such files
00207          * if it's unduly awkward to do so.
00208          *
00209          * Note that the expression eh.e_phoff + i*eh.e_phentsize is 
00210          * mandated by the ELF standard - we use sizeof(ph) to load,
00211          * because that's the structure we know, but the file on disk
00212          * might have a larger structure, so we must use e_phentsize
00213          * to find where the phdr starts.
00214          */
00215 
00216         for (i=0; i<eh.e_phnum; i++) {
00217                 off_t offset = eh.e_phoff + i*eh.e_phentsize;
00218                 uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, &ph, sizeof(ph), offset, UIO_READ);
00219 
00220                 result = VOP_READ(v, &ku);
00221                 if (result) {
00222                         return result;
00223                 }
00224 
00225                 if (ku.uio_resid != 0) {
00226                         /* short read; problem with executable? */
00227                         kprintf("ELF: short read on phdr - file truncated?\n");
00228                         return ENOEXEC;
00229                 }
00230 
00231                 switch (ph.p_type) {
00232                     case PT_NULL: /* skip */ continue;
00233                     case PT_PHDR: /* skip */ continue;
00234                     case PT_MIPS_REGINFO: /* skip */ continue;
00235                     case PT_LOAD: break;
00236                     default:
00237                         kprintf("loadelf: unknown segment type %d\n", 
00238                                 ph.p_type);
00239                         return ENOEXEC;
00240                 }
00241 
00242                 result = as_define_region(curthread->t_addrspace,
00243                                           ph.p_vaddr, ph.p_memsz,
00244                                           ph.p_flags & PF_R,
00245                                           ph.p_flags & PF_W,
00246                                           ph.p_flags & PF_X);
00247                 if (result) {
00248                         return result;
00249                 }
00250         }
00251 
00252         result = as_prepare_load(curthread->t_addrspace);
00253         if (result) {
00254                 return result;
00255         }
00256 
00257         /*
00258          * Now actually load each segment.
00259          */
00260 
00261         for (i=0; i<eh.e_phnum; i++) {
00262                 off_t offset = eh.e_phoff + i*eh.e_phentsize;
00263                 uio_kinit(&iov, &ku, &ph, sizeof(ph), offset, UIO_READ);
00264 
00265                 result = VOP_READ(v, &ku);
00266                 if (result) {
00267                         return result;
00268                 }
00269 
00270                 if (ku.uio_resid != 0) {
00271                         /* short read; problem with executable? */
00272                         kprintf("ELF: short read on phdr - file truncated?\n");
00273                         return ENOEXEC;
00274                 }
00275 
00276                 switch (ph.p_type) {
00277                     case PT_NULL: /* skip */ continue;
00278                     case PT_PHDR: /* skip */ continue;
00279                     case PT_MIPS_REGINFO: /* skip */ continue;
00280                     case PT_LOAD: break;
00281                     default:
00282                         kprintf("loadelf: unknown segment type %d\n", 
00283                                 ph.p_type);
00284                         return ENOEXEC;
00285                 }
00286 
00287                 result = load_segment(v, ph.p_offset, ph.p_vaddr, 
00288                                       ph.p_memsz, ph.p_filesz,
00289                                       ph.p_flags & PF_X);
00290                 if (result) {
00291                         return result;
00292                 }
00293         }
00294 
00295         result = as_complete_load(curthread->t_addrspace);
00296         if (result) {
00297                 return result;
00298         }
00299 
00300         *entrypoint = eh.e_entry;
00301 
00302         return 0;
00303 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
